beats.title: Ambush in the Pit
beats.boundaryReason: The session opens with the party sliding into the arena chamber, the beast calling up living snow around each hero, and the group scrambling through the first exchange while they identify the encounter and try to stabilize their positions.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets drStart to 1730-01-25
the party resumes directly from the prior cliffhanger with no sleep, camp, or other day transition before initiative
nothing in the opening combat setup suggests a change from the same continuous delve

beats.title: Cracking the Snow Guard
beats.boundaryReason: After the opening scramble, the fight shifts into a tactical middle phase as the party protects Kalima, discovers that the living snows absorb or redirect many attacks, and starts to pivot toward spells, force, and focused pressure on the beast.
dateEvidence: this beat follows immediately from the opening initiative order with no break in the battle
Kalima remains hidden in the same chamber and the party is still operating under the same uninterrupted combat scene
the transcript gives no rest, sunset, or next-day cue during this phase

beats.title: Turning the Echoes
beats.boundaryReason: The party finally decodes the living snows enough to start killing them efficiently, with Justas recasting Spirit Guardians, Eolo's attacks landing cleanly, and the fight swinging away from survival mode toward control of the chamber.
dateEvidence: the combat continues seamlessly from the prior beat in the same arena with no interruption
the only progression is tactical momentum within the same encounter, not a change in day
session.yaml still places the active session within 1730-01-25 at this point

beats.title: The Beast Falls
beats.boundaryReason: With most of the echoes stripped away, the last combat phase becomes a direct collapse of the beast's defenses until the party kills it and the chamber itself begins to change.
dateEvidence: this beat is the final uninterrupted portion of the same combat encounter
the environmental transformation begins immediately on the beast's death with no pause, rest, or sleep between phases
no transcript evidence advances the date beyond 1730-01-25 before the kill

beats.title: Through the Earthen Exit
beats.boundaryReason: After the beast dies, the room sheds its frost, Kalima panics toward the new tunnel, the party grabs only a few facts and trophies, and the scene shifts completely when they crawl out onto the cliffside overlooking Melusa and dispatch the fae scout toward the ice palace.
dateEvidence: the party emerges from the tunnel into a scene the DM explicitly frames as late afternoon
the tunnel crawl takes only about ten minutes, keeping this beat on the same day as the combat
there is still no sleep, long rest, or next-morning cue after leaving the labyrinth chamber

beats.title: Hidden Into Melusa
beats.boundaryReason: Kalima rushes the group up the switchbacks, warns them to stay unseen, and leads them through the eerie ledge village into Nura and Khalid's house, where the surviving residents receive them as strange but potentially sacred outsiders.
dateEvidence: the DM continues to describe the exterior ledge as late afternoon while they approach the village
the approach, stealth entry, and move into Nura and Khalid's house are one continuous scene with no rest break
the transcript still gives no signal of the date advancing beyond 1730-01-25

beats.title: House of Endurance
beats.boundaryReason: Inside the safe house, the party learns how Melusa understands Kestavo, sacrifice, sunset, and survival, while the villagers reveal just enough of their cosmology and fears for the players to realize how deeply isolated the settlement has become.
dateEvidence: the villagers describe the sun as still hanging in its strange prolonged cycle, and the environment remains locked in late-afternoon light
the conversation flows directly from arrival and continues before the gong-driven sleep cycle begins
although session.yaml ends on 1730-01-26, the transcript has not yet shown an on-stage transition into the next day

beats.title: Portals and Night Watch
beats.boundaryReason: Once the gong rings, the session turns into layered reconnaissance: Ekko scouts the village and the guarded mirror-gash, Finnan uses clairvoyance to inspect the palace and the forming ice bridge, and the party ends by choosing to hide, rest, and face the consequences tomorrow.
dateEvidence: the gong marks the village's sleep cycle and the scene explicitly shifts into night-time planning
the party discusses bedding down, taking a long rest, and picking things up tomorrow, but no next-morning action occurs on stage before the session ends
keeping the beat on 1730-01-25 matches the transcripted events even though session.yaml allows the overnight span to extend into 1730-01-26
